question.text: Scenario: The Kapoor family consists of Mr. Rajiv (45, software engineer, gross salary Rs. 15 lakh p.a.) and Mrs. Neha (42, freelance designer, annual income Rs. 6 lakh). Their monthly breakdown is: Gross salary (Rajiv) Rs. 1,25,000, Freelance income (Neha) Rs. 50,000, Employer PF contribution Rs. 1,800, Employee PF contribution Rs. 1,800. Mandatory expenses: Tax deductions Rs. 12,000, Loan EMIs Rs. 35,000. Essential expenses: Groceries Rs. 15,000, Children's education Rs. 20,000, Rent Rs. 25,000, Utilities Rs. 8,000, Transportation Rs. 10,000. Discretionary expenses: Entertainment Rs. 8,000, Lifestyle Rs. 12,000, Annual holiday fund Rs. 5,000. Their assets include: Equity MFs Rs. 22 lakh, FDs Rs. 10 lakh, PPF Rs. 8 lakh, PF balance Rs. 14 lakh, Savings bank Rs. 2 lakh, Gold jewellery Rs. 6 lakh, Car (3 years old) Rs. 5 lakh. Liabilities: Home loan Rs. 18 lakh, Car loan Rs. 2,50,000. Question 2 of 5: What is the Kapoor family's total financial assets, total physical assets, and financial assets ratio?

answer.text: Financial Rs. 56 lakh, Physical Rs. 11 lakh, Ratio 83.6%
Financial Rs. 50 lakh, Physical Rs. 17 lakh, Ratio 74.6%
Financial Rs. 56 lakh, Physical Rs. 6 lakh, Ratio 90.3%
Financial Rs. 42 lakh, Physical Rs. 11 lakh, Ratio 79.2%

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Financial assets = Equity MFs 22 + FDs 10 + PPF 8 + PF 14 + Savings bank 2 = Rs. 56 lakh. Physical assets = Gold 6 + Car 5 = Rs. 11 lakh. Total = 67 lakh. Financial assets ratio = 56/67 = 83.6%. Note: Self-occupied home is excluded from assets in conservative estimation. **The Trap:** Including the home value (which isn't mentioned as an investment property) or excluding PF from financial assets changes the ratio significantly. **Cross-Exam Context:** Financial vs physical asset classification and ratio is tested in X-A (Ch 3). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 83.6% in financial assets is a healthy ratio — financial assets offer better liquidity and flexibility for goal funding than physical assets.
